course_objectives: 1. To understand complex variable. 2. To apply concepts of Fourier and Z-transform in signal processing. 3. To study wave and diffusion equations in Cartesian, cylindrical, and polar coordinates.

topics.topic: 1.1 Review of complex numbers — polar form, modulus, argument, DeMoivre's theorem
topics.note: Asked as intro/short questions. DeMoivre's theorem used in deriving roots and powers.

topics.topic: 1.2 Functions of complex variables — definition, limits, continuity
topics.note: Foundation for analyticity; often combined with Cauchy-Riemann equations.

topics.topic: 1.3 Cauchy-Riemann equations — necessary and sufficient conditions for analyticity
topics.note: One of the highest frequency topics. Asked to check analyticity and as prerequisite for harmonic functions.

topics.topic: 1.4 Harmonic functions — harmonic conjugate, construction of analytic function f(z) = u + iv
topics.note: MOST FREQUENTLY ASKED TOPIC in Unit 1. Appears in virtually every paper. Milne-Thomson method included.

topics.topic: 1.5 Conformal mappings — definition, angle-preserving property, standard maps w = e^{iα}z, w = 1/z, w = z²
topics.note: Image of regions under transformations. Triangular/circular region mapping asked repeatedly.

topics.topic: 1.6 Bilinear (Möbius) transformation — definition, fixed points, cross-ratio
topics.note: Finding bilinear map from three point-pairs and image of unit circle/real axis.

topics.topic: 1.7 Cauchy integral theorem and complex line integrals
topics.note: Statement, conditions, direct evaluation of contour integrals.

topics.topic: 1.8 Cauchy's integral formula and its extension for higher-order derivatives
topics.note: State-and-prove plus evaluation questions. One of the most tested topics in complex integration.

topics.topic: 1.9 Taylor's series of a complex function — convergence and radius
topics.note: Expansion around a regular point; less frequently asked as a standalone question.

topics.topic: 1.10 Laurent's series expansion in annular regions
topics.note: Expanding in multiple annular domains (e.g., |z|<1, 1<|z|<2, |z|>2) is a common exam pattern.

topics.topic: 1.11 Zeros, singularities, poles — isolated, removable, essential; classification
topics.note: Identifying type of singularity and order of pole. Often paired with residue calculation.

topics.topic: 1.12 Calculation of residues at poles — Cauchy's residue theorem and evaluation of real integrals
topics.note: State-and-prove residue theorem + definite integral evaluation. Second most tested integration topic.

topics.topic: 2.1 Z-transform — definition, one-sided and two-sided Z-transform, standard transforms, ROC
topics.note: Basic Z-transform table, properties (linearity, scaling, time-reversal) regularly asked.

topics.topic: 2.2 Linear Time-Invariant system, unit impulse function, properties of Z-transform
topics.note: Convolution theorem, shifting theorem (first and second), initial and final value theorems.

topics.topic: 2.3 Shifting theorems — first and second shifting theorems with applications
topics.note: Z{aⁿ cos bt}, Z{aⁿ sin bt} and related forms. Appears in nearly every paper.

topics.topic: 2.4 Inverse Z-transform by residue and partial fraction methods; long division
topics.note: Partial fraction decomposition and power series (long division) are both asked.

topics.topic: 2.5 Parseval's theorem and convolution theorem for Z-transform
topics.note: Less frequently asked as standalone; often part of larger Z-transform questions.

topics.topic: 2.6 Solution of difference equations using Z-transform
topics.note: MOST FREQUENTLY TESTED in Unit 2. Solving y_{n+2} + ay_{n+1} + by_n = f(n) with initial conditions appears in nearly every paper.

topics.topic: 3.1 Fourier series in complex form — derivation from Fourier integral theorem
topics.note: Derivation of Fourier integral from Fourier series; often asked as a first sub-part.

topics.topic: 3.2 Fourier integral representation — Fourier sine integral and cosine integral
topics.note: Evaluating ∫₀^∞ [...] dω using a suitable function. One of the two most tested Fourier topics.

topics.topic: 3.3 Fourier transform — definition, linearity, basic properties, Gaussian function
topics.note: F{e^{-x²/2}} and similar; properties (time-shift, scaling, convolution) regularly asked.

topics.topic: 3.4 Fourier sine and cosine transforms — computation and applications
topics.note: F_s and F_c of e^{-x}, e^{-mx}; then proving standard definite integral results. Equally most-tested with 3.2.

topics.topic: 3.5 Inverse Fourier transform and Parseval's identity — evaluation of definite integrals
topics.note: ∫₀^∞ 1/(1+x²)² dx = π/4 via Parseval, and related problems.

topics.topic: 4.1 Partial differential equations — definition, examples, classification, order and degree
topics.note: Introductory theory; less commonly asked as a full question.

topics.topic: 4.2 Method of separation of variables — solving PDEs with boundary conditions
topics.note: Applied to u_{xx} − u_{xy} = 0 and similar. Fundamental tool for 4.3–4.6.

topics.topic: 4.3 Derivation and solution of wave equation (one and two dimensional) — vibrating string
topics.note: MOST FREQUENTLY TESTED in Unit 4. Derive 1D wave equation + solve with BCs and IC appearing in nearly every paper.

topics.topic: 4.4 Wave equation by D'Alembert's method
topics.note: Alternative closed-form solution; asked occasionally as a second sub-part.

topics.topic: 4.5 Derivation and solution of heat equation (one and two dimensional) — temperature distribution
topics.note: Derive 1D heat equation + solve for u(x,t) with ends at zero temperature. Appears in nearly every paper.

topics.topic: 4.6 Laplace equation — Cartesian, polar coordinates; expressing ∇²u in polar form
topics.note: Deriving ∇²u in polar coordinates is a very common question. Cylindrical/spherical form: statement only.

topics.topic: 4.7 Helmholtz equation and engineering applications of PDEs
topics.note: 2D boundary-value problems; Helmholtz equation F_{xx}+F_{yy}+k²F = 0 solved occasionally.

topics.topic: 5.1 Quadric surfaces — ellipsoid, hyperboloid, paraboloid, cylinder, cone; standard equations and sketches
topics.note: Write equation, sketch, identify axes of symmetry. Asked as short-answer or sub-part.

topics.topic: 5.2 Tangent line and tangent plane on the space curve — tangent vector, unit tangent vector
topics.note: Finding tangent vector r⃗'(t) and unit tangent T̂ at a given point. Appears in most recent papers.

references: 1. E. Kreyszig, Advanced Engineering Mathematics, 8th edition, Wiley-Eastern Publication, New Delhi.
2. H. K. Dass & R. Verma, Higher Engineering Mathematics, First edition, S. Chand & Company Limited, New Delhi.
3. Digital Signal Processing: J.G. Proakis, Prentice Hall of India.
4. V. Sundaran, R. Bala Subramanayam, K.L. Laxminarayanam, Engineering Mathematics, Volume II.
5. A.V. Oppenheim, Discrete-Time Signal Processing, Prentice Hall, India Limited, 1990.
6. K. Ogata, Discrete-Time Control System, Prentice Hall, India Limited, 1993.
